free_text: Interaction of loosely bound radioactive 7Be and
stable 7Li with 9Be
15 UD pelletron accelerator and
radioactive beam facility were used to perform the
measurements.
7Li primary beam from the pelletron
was incident on a thin (12 um) polypropylene foil,
mounted on a  rotating/linearly moving target
assembly to produce 7Be
9Be target of thickness 1.368 mg/cm2.
7Be+9Be system:
Quasielastically scattered particles were identified
by means of a telescope consisting of an ionization
chamber followed by two large-area position-sensitive,
50 mm x 50 mm, Si-surface barrier detectors (PSSD, Si-
SB), placed adjacent to each other.
The pressure of the P10 gas in the ionization chamber
was maintained at 76 +- 0.5 mbar.
A 500 um thick Si-SB detector, for normalisation, was
mounted and periodically introduced in the beam path
to measure 7Be counts.
A dE-E Si telescope (dE = 300 um, E = 2 mm) was placed
at +30 degree with respect to the beam axis in the
7Be production chamber to monitor the elastic recoil
protons.
7Li+9Be system:
The measurements were carried in 1.5 m diameter
general purpose scattering chamber using dE-E solid-
state surface barrier telescopes, mounted on two
movable arms.
The angular separation between the Si-detectors was
6 degree.
Fusion measurements:
Fusion measurements were performed by identifying
evaporated alpha-particles at some of the backward
angles using a dE-E telescope(13 um + 300 um),
subtending a solid angle of 0.078 msr.
Inclusive measurements:
Inclusive measurements of energy spectra and angular
distributions of the light reaction products, alpha,
t, d, p, were also performed, using a sufficiently
thick telescope (150 um+ 3 um).
The low-energy alpha-particles were detected using
another telescope (25 um + 300 um).
An overall normalisation was obtained at each energy
by placing a monitor(Si-300 um) detector at +10
degree to the beam.
The error in the normalisation procedure
plus the statistical and systematic errors result in
an overall uncertainty of ~12% in the cross-sections.
Approved by Dr.S.Verma in 2009
A.B.Pawar, V.K.Mulik, B.Lalremruata

The fusion cross sections were extracted by measuring
alpha-energy spectra (integrated over 1 MeV energy
bins).
Fusion measurements were performed by identifying
evaporated alpha-particles at some of the backward
angles using a DE-E telescope (13 um + 300 um),
subtending a solid angle of 0.078 msr.
The error in normalisation procedure plus
the statistical and systematic errors result in an
over all uncertainty of ~12% in the cross sections.

fig.1
The elastically scattered particles were identified
by means of a telescope consisting of an ionization
chamber followed by two large area position
sensitive (50 mm x 50 mm) Si-surface barrier
detectors (PSSD, Si-SB), placed adjacent to each
other.
The pressure of the P10 gas in the ionization chamber
was maintained at 76 +/- 0.5 mbar, throughout the
experiment.
In the focal plane chamber, at the end of the movable
target ladder, a Si-SB detector(normalisation
detector) was mounted and periodically introduced in
the beam path, during the experiment, to measure 7Be
counts.
Another Si-SB detector was placed at +30 deg with
respect to the beam axis, in the 7Be production
chamber to monitor the recoil protons.
The error in normalisation procedure plus
the statistical and systematic errors result in an
over all uncertainty of ~12% in the cross sections.
Data were received from Dr.S.Verma

The low energy alpha particles were detected using
